name: Thyme Pesto Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup thyme pesto or purchased pesto sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
2. Rub the softened butter over the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Mix the breadcrumbs with 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese. Coat the pan with the crumb mixture.
3. Using an electric mixer, beat the cream cheese, ricotta, remaining 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese, salt, and cayenne in a large bowl until fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Transfer half of the mixture to a medium bowl. Mix pesto into the remaining half.
5. Pour the pesto mixture into the prepared pan, smoothing the top. Carefully spoon the plain mixture on top; smooth. Sprinkle with pine nuts.
6. Bake the cheesecake until the center is no longer wobbly when shaken, about 45 minutes. Transfer to a rack and cool completely. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and chill overnight.
7. Run a small sharp knife around the pan sides to loosen the cheesecake; remove the sides. Transfer the cake to a platter or raised cake plate. Garnish with basil sprigs and serve with crackers.
